verificationNote: Educational scaffold. Lessons cite IEC 62443, NIST SP 800-82, MITRE ATT&CK for ICS, and NACSA Act 854 — verify any operational decision against the authoritative source. No lesson here substitutes for sector-specific guidance from a qualified OT security professional.
description: Four-tier learning path for OT security. Each tier ends with a checkpoint. Each lesson links to the data layer in this repo so theory and reference live one click apart.
howToUse: Read top to bottom. Skip ahead only if you can pass the previous tier's checkpoint. Every claim that names a control, SR, or incident is clickable — use those links to dig into the underlying data.

tiers.name: Orientation
tiers.audience: Anyone new to OT — IT engineers, GRC professionals, executives.
tiers.outcome: Can name Purdue levels, explain why patching ≠ IT, list five canonical OT incidents, and articulate what an IDMZ is and why it matters.

lessons.title: Why OT is different from IT
lessons.summary: Confidentiality vs availability vs safety. The triage order changes everything.

lessons.title: The Purdue Model in 10 minutes
lessons.summary: Levels 0–5 plus the IDMZ. What lives where and what talks to what.

lessons.title: Six incidents you must know (incl. Monterrey 2026)
lessons.summary: Stuxnet, Ukraine, TRITON, Colonial Pipeline, Oldsmar, Monterrey — what each one taught the field, and what AI-augmented recon changed.

lessons.title: IEC 62443 and NACSA Act 854 in 15 minutes
lessons.summary: The standard, the law, and how they fit together for Malaysian NCII operators.

lessons.title: Checkpoint — orientation self-test
lessons.summary: Eight questions. If you can't answer them, re-read the relevant lesson before moving on.

tiers.name: Practitioner
tiers.audience: IT security professional newly responsible for an OT environment.
tiers.outcome: Can run a passive asset inventory, map devices to Purdue levels, identify the IDMZ gap in an existing environment, and draft a remote-access policy from the gold-standard template.

lessons.title: Lab — passive asset discovery from PCAP
lessons.summary: Use the included pcap-analyzer.py to extract OT assets from a capture. Why active scanning kills PLCs.

lessons.title: Lab — stand up a Modbus honeypot
lessons.summary: Watch what an attacker sees on port 502. Use to baseline what normal Modbus traffic looks like.

lessons.title: Map your assets to the Purdue model
lessons.summary: Take the asset list from Lesson 1 and produce a defensible Purdue diagram.

lessons.title: Find the IDMZ gap
lessons.summary: Walk a network diagram. Identify every IT-to-OT path that doesn't traverse the IDMZ.

lessons.title: Lab — draft a remote-access policy from the template
lessons.summary: Customise the IAM gold-standard template for a vendor-heavy plant.

lessons.title: Checkpoint — practitioner deliverables review
lessons.summary: Five artefacts you should now own. If any are missing, you skipped something.

tiers.name: Programme Builder
tiers.audience: New OT security manager, NACSA NCII operator standing up a programme from scratch.
tiers.outcome: Can stand up the 10 B.A.S.I.C. S.T.A.R.T. pillars, produce the NACSA-required artefacts, and run a tabletop exercise that an auditor will accept.

lessons.title: The 10 pillars — sequencing and dependencies
lessons.summary: Why B (Backup) precedes everything. Why T (Teamwork) is last but not least.

lessons.title: How to use a pillar workbook (read this once)
lessons.summary: The Discover → Plan → Produce → KPI pattern — applied per pillar.

lessons.title: Pillar B — Backup & Recovery (3-2-1-1-0 for OT)
lessons.summary: Build a backup programme that survives ransomware AND respects PLC restoration sequencing.

lessons.title: Pillar I — Incident Response (with NACSA s26 timing)
lessons.summary: Build the IR plan and the s26 6-hour notification flow. Run a TTX from the included scenarios.

lessons.title: Running an OT tabletop exercise (TTX)
lessons.summary: Scenario design, engineering injects, the IT/OT severance question, AAR.

lessons.title: Checkpoint — programme readiness review
lessons.summary: 10 artefacts, one per pillar. The Board-pack you would defend in a regulatory inspection.

tiers.name: Auditor / Assessor
tiers.audience: NACSA-licensed auditor, internal auditor, IESP assessor.
tiers.outcome: Can perform an SL-2 / SL-3 zone assessment, complete the FAT/SAT checklist as evidence, and produce an audit package that maps each finding to its IEC 62443 SR and NACSA section.

lessons.title: Anatomy of an OT audit package
lessons.summary: Work programme + PBC list + finding template + rating methodology — and what each one is for.

lessons.title: Evidence-gathering by domain
lessons.summary: Use evidence/index.json. What good looks like, what common gaps look like, how to verify in 30 minutes per domain.

lessons.title: Walking the FAT/SAT checklist on a real handover
lessons.summary: Use the included FAT/SAT checklist on the worked sample report. What to accept, what to reject.

lessons.title: Writing a finding that maps to SR + NACSA
lessons.summary: Every finding cites its IEC 62443 SR and its NACSA section. The finding template enforces this.

lessons.title: Checkpoint — produce an audit package
lessons.summary: Apply the workflow end-to-end on a sector of your choice. Self-grade against the rubric.
